title: clematis crispa var. walteri
intro: Clematis crispa var. walteri, also known as Virginia clematis, is a flowering plant native to the southeastern United States and is characterized by its fragrant white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of clematis crispa var. walteri include its ability to aid in the treatment of skin irritations, wounds, and infections, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of fever. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve infusions or tinctures made from the leaves and stems, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
